results.description: A collection of MCP servers.

results.name: Developer-Y/cs-video-courses
results.description: List of Computer Science courses with video lectures.

results.name: bradtraversy/design-resources-for-developers
results.description: Curated list of design and UI resources from stock photos, web templates, CSS frameworks, UI libraries, tools and much more

results.name: ComposioHQ/awesome-claude-skills
results.description: A curated list of awesome Claude Skills, resources, and tools for customizing Claude AI workflows

results.name: charlax/professional-programming
results.description: A collection of learning resources for curious software engineers

results.name: VoltAgent/awesome-openclaw-skills
results.description: The awesome collection of OpenClaw skills. 5,400+ skills filtered and categorized from the official OpenClaw Skills Registry.🦞

results.name: ruvnet/ruflo
results.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features    enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration

results.name: KeygraphHQ/shannon
results.description: Shannon Lite is an autonomous, white-box AI pentester for web applications and APIs. It analyzes your source code, identifies attack vectors, and executes real exploits to prove vulnerabilities before they reach production.

results.name: HKUDS/LightRAG
results.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"

results.name: HKUDS/CLI-Anything
results.description: "CLI-Anything: Making ALL Software Agent-Native" -- CLI-Hub: https://clianything.cc/

results.name: microsoft/graphrag
results.description: A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system

results.name: wilsonfreitas/awesome-quant
results.description: A curated list of insanely awesome libraries, packages and resources for Quants (Quantitative Finance)

results.name: Strivin0311/long-llms-learning
results.description: A repository sharing the literatures about long-context large language models, including the methodologies and the evaluation benchmarks

results.name: khromov/svelte-bench
results.description: An LLM benchmark for Svelte 5 based on the OpenAI methodology from OpenAIs paper "Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code".

results.name: tryDML/txt-2-sql-benchmark
results.description: An app and set of methodologies designed to evaluate the performance of various Large Language Models (LLMs) on the text-to-SQL task. Our goal is to offer a standardized way to measure how well these models can generate SQL queries from natural language descriptions

results.name: rawalraj022/aiprl-llm-intelligence-report
results.description: This repo to establishes (AIPRL-LIR) framework for Large Language Model overall evaluation and analysis through systematic monthly intelligence reports. Unlike typical AI research papers or commercial reports. It provides structured insights into AI model performance, benchmarking methodologies, Multi-hosting provider analysis, industry trends ....

results.name: sebuzdugan/frai-benchmark
results.description: FRAI Benchmark: Future Responsible AI Evaluation A consensus-based safety and compliance benchmark for SOTA LLMs (DeepSeek, Grok, GPT-5). Uses a "Panel of Experts" methodology where multiple frontier models judge response quality to ensure non-biased, high-fidelity safety scores

results.name: caspiankeyes/AART-AI-Adversarial-Research-Toolkit
results.description: AART provides security researchers, AI labs, and red teams with a structured framework for conducting thorough adversarial evaluations of LLM systems. The framework implements a multi-dimensional assessment methodology that systematically probes model boundaries, quantifies security vulnerabilities and benchmarks defensive robustness in frontier AI

results.name: Palmerschallon/Dharma_Code
results.description: Polyglot ontological activations for LLM systems. 68 terms from 20+ traditions mapped to computational patterns, plus 10 algorithms native to the ontology that have no equivalents in standard CS. Includes benchmark suite and a documented evaluation methodology confound finding.

results.name: CartesianXR7/bellwether
results.description: The cost-and-failure-mode benchmark for LLM agents. Methodology plus Python package for honest, reproducible cross-provider agent evaluation.

results.name: mukulchhabra23/enterprise-rag-eval
results.description: Case-aware evaluation framework for enterprise RAG systems using LLM-as-a-judge methodology. Includes reproducible pipeline, metrics, and benchmarking tools.

results.name: aprilatkinson/lab-llm-as-judge-evaluation
results.description: LLM evaluation lab using LLM-as-Judge methodology, custom benchmarks, and Python implementation.

results.name: davis9001/deno-fresh-llm-benchmark
results.description: UNFINISHED An LLM benchmark for Deno Fresh based on the OpenAI methodology from OpenAIs paper "Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code". Shamelessly stolen from https://github.com/khromov/svelte-bench

results.name: rukun-dou/DB-MMLU
results.description: A benchmark to evaluate LLMs' suceptibility to hallucination derived from MMLU using Deception-Based Benchmarking methodology.

results.name: fardaniys/LLMProject
results.description: This portfolio demonstrates advanced capability in evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) and agent systems using structured, rubric-based methodologies aligned with real-world AI evaluation workflows (e.g., RLHF, benchmarking, and QA pipelines).

results.name: swissprismia/llm-political-bias-bench
results.description: Benchmark suite for evaluating political bias in LLM models using Likert-scale scoring and pairwise ranking, with an LLM-as-judge methodology.

results.name: Laurene-v/replicatingPET
results.description: This is the code used for the paper "A Looming Replication Crisis in Evaluating Behavior in Language Models? Evidence and Solutions", https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.20303 . It is used to easily replicate prompt engineering techniques on selected benchmarks, LLMs, and verification methodologies.

results.name: khromov/svelte-bench
results.description: An LLM benchmark for Svelte 5 based on the OpenAI methodology from OpenAIs paper "Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code".

results.description: An app and set of methodologies designed to evaluate the performance of various Large Language Models (LLMs) on the text-to-SQL task. Our goal is to offer a standardized way to measure how well these models can generate SQL queries from natural language descriptions

Unlike typical AI research papers or commercial reports. It provides structured insights into AI model performance, benchmarking methodologies, Multi-hosting provider analysis, industry trends ....

results.name: avnlp/rag-model-training
results.description: Training code for advanced RAG techniques - Adaptive-RAG, Corrective RAG, RQ-RAG, Self-RAG, Agentic RAG, and ReZero. Reproduces paper methodologies to fine-tune LLMs via SFT and GRPO for adaptive retrieval, corrective evaluation, query refinement, self-reflection, and agentic search behaviors.

results.name: sebuzdugan/frai-benchmark
results.description: FRAI Benchmark: Future Responsible AI Evaluation A consensus-based safety and compliance benchmark for SOTA LLMs (DeepSeek, Grok, GPT-5). Uses a "Panel of Experts" methodology where multiple frontier models judge response quality to ensure non-biased, high-fidelity safety scores

results.name: CakeCrusher/cultural_evolution
results.description: implements the methodology outlined in the paper *Cultural Evolution of Cooperation among LLM Agents*. The paper explores whether a society of large language model (LLM) agents can develop cooperative norms through cultural evolution, using the classic *Donor Game*. The goal is to evaluate multi-agent interaction dynamics

results.name: e-xperiments/datawarden
results.description: This repository is dedicated to providing cutting-edge tools and methodologies to evaluate and curate datasets specifically designed for Large Language Models (LLMs). Leveraging the capabilities of LLMs themselves, combined with programmatic best practices, our toolkit ensures a robust evaluation and refinement process for your datasets.

results.name: 5krus/HyTE
results.description: LLM-based Hypothesize-Test-Evaluate methodology.

results.name: nsatpute/Measuring-Fairness-in-LLMs
results.description: This project creates a framework to evaluate and ensure fairness in Large Language Models (LLMs). It focuses on detecting and mitigating biases through model testing, red teaming with adversarial prompts, and robust scoring methodologies. The aim is to ensure AI-generated content is ethical, fair, and legally compliant across diverse use cases.

results.name: EladAriel/llm-eval-plugin
results.description: A comprehensive toolkit for evaluating and building LLM applications, combining systematic evaluation methodology with practical RAG implementation.

results.name: arthurcerveira/gilBERTo-Language-Model
results.description: Methodology to pre-train and evaluate a LLM to the Portuguese language
